> Generated by `python app.py --eval`. Fill the **Ground-truth answer** and judge **Retrieval quality** / **Response accuracy** yourself — do not take the auto-run as a passing grade.

## Q1. How do I get into a class that's already full?

**Ground-truth answer:** _[fill in from your documents]_

**System response:** To get into a class that's already full, try the following steps: 

1. Log in to MyMav the minute your enrollment appointment time opens, as full classes turn over fast in the first hour [02_registration_tips.txt].
2. Join the waitlist in MyMav, even if it looks long, as waitlists move a lot during the first two weeks [02_registration_tips.txt].
3. Email the professor before the semester starts, being short and polite, and ask if they'll sign an add/override [02_registration_tips.txt].
4. Physically attend the first lecture, as seats open up when no-shows get dropped, and professors are more likely to add you in person [02_registration_tips.txt].

Additionally, as one student noted, don't assume a full class is impossible to get into, as they were able to get into a class after delaying graduation a semester [05_wish_i_knew.txt].

**Sources cited:** 02_registration_tips.txt, 02_registration_tips.txt, 05_wish_i_knew.txt, 02_registration_tips.txt

**Chunks retrieved:** 02_registration_tips.txt#0(0.675), 02_registration_tips.txt#2(0.552), 05_wish_i_knew.txt#1(0.524), 02_registration_tips.txt#1(0.521), 09_semester_logistics.txt#2(0.518)

**Response accuracy:** Accurate / Partially accurate / Inaccurate

## Q2. Where are the best quiet places to study on campus?

**Ground-truth answer:** _[fill in from your documents]_

**System response:** For quiet study spots, consider the upper floors of the Central Library, as they are designated quiet zones [04_study_spots.txt]. The Architecture building (CAPPA) is also a good option, especially at night, with its big studio spaces being dead quiet [04_study_spots.txt]. Additionally, the Science and Engineering Library is smaller and less crowded than Central, making it a good spot for solo studying [04_study_spots.txt]. If you prefer a non-library setting, the second floor of the University Center has quiet corners in the late afternoon [04_study_spots.txt].

**Sources cited:** 04_study_spots.txt, 04_study_spots.txt, 04_study_spots.txt

## Q3. What's the best way to get around campus without a car?

**Ground-truth answer:** _[fill in from your documents]_

**System response:** The best way to get around campus without a car is to use the Maverick Shuttle (the "Mav Mover") which runs several routes connecting the residence halls, the main academic core, and nearby apartment complexes, and is free with your student ID [03_campus_transit.txt]. Additionally, campus itself is compact and can be walked end to end in about 15 minutes, so most students never need wheels to get to class [03_campus_transit.txt]. If you live in the apartments just south of campus, bringing a bike can also be helpful [03_campus_transit.txt]. Living on campus is also a good option as you're a walk from class and the shuttle hits the halls [10_dorm_advice.txt].

**Sources cited:** 03_campus_transit.txt, 03_campus_transit.txt, 10_dorm_advice.txt, 03_campus_transit.txt, 03_campus_transit.txt

## Q4. What freshman mistake do upperclassmen warn against the most?

**Ground-truth answer:** _[fill in from your documents]_

**System response:** Upperclassmen warn against buying a parking permit that isn't needed, as freshmen often drop a few hundred dollars on a permit and barely use it [08_freshman_mistakes.txt], [05_wish_i_knew.txt]. This is mentioned in both a comment on the freshman mistakes thread [08_freshman_mistakes.txt] and in a student blog post about things they wish they knew before their first year [05_wish_i_knew.txt].

## Q5. Where can I find cheap food near campus?

**Ground-truth answer:** _[fill in from your documents]_

**System response:** You can find cheap food near campus along the strip on Cooper St and UTA Blvd, which has tons of fast food and taco spots within walking distance, most under $10 [06_cheap_food.txt]. Additionally, the Mav Express markets on campus have grab-and-go options that take meal-plan dining dollars [06_cheap_food.txt]. Pho and ramen places just off campus are also a good option, with one big bowl being like two meals if you save half [06_cheap_food.txt]. Some local spots also offer student discounts if you show your Mav ID [06_cheap_food.txt].

**Sources cited:** 06_cheap_food.txt, 06_cheap_food.txt, 06_cheap_food.txt
